title: Mac and cheese
subtitle: Baked macaroni in a cheddar béchamel, with a crisp breadcrumb crust.
excerpt: Small pasta cooked in a sharp cheddar and mustard sauce, baked until bubbling under a golden breadcrumb crust. A proper American mac and cheese, not the packet version. Serves four.
sourceNotes: American baked macaroni and cheese. The dish has roots in 18th-century American cooking; Thomas Jefferson is often associated with introducing it to American diners after encountering it in France. Mary Randolph's The Virginia Housewife (1824, public domain) contains an early American recipe for macaroni and cheese.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble the day before and refrigerate unbaked. Add 10 minutes to the baking time from cold.

content.text: The key to a good mac and cheese is a sauce with enough body to coat the pasta without seizing into a stiff block in the oven. The roux must be cooked out fully before the milk goes in, and the cheese must go off the heat to prevent it from splitting. A little mustard in the sauce is not optional; it amplifies the cheddar.
Cook {{pasta-dried}} of macaroni in a large pan of well-salted boiling water for 2 minutes less than the packet says. Drain and set aside.

Melt {{unsalted-butter}} of butter in the same saucepan over medium heat. Add {{plain-flour}} of flour and stir for 2 minutes. Gradually pour in {{whole-milk}} of milk, whisking constantly, until smooth. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring often, until the sauce is thick and coats the back of a spoon. Remove from the heat. Stir in {{cheddar}} of grated cheddar, {{english-mustard}} of English mustard, {{nutmeg-ground}} of nutmeg, {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt and {{black-pepper}} of pepper until the cheese is melted and smooth.

Heat the oven to 190°C. Stir the pasta into the cheese sauce and pour into the baking dish. Mix {{breadcrumbs-dried}} of breadcrumbs with the remaining {{cheddar}} of cheddar and scatter evenly over the top. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes until the top is golden and the sauce is bubbling at the edges. Leave to rest for 5 minutes before serving.

items.cause: Cheese added to a boiling sauce, or pre-grated cheese with anti-caking agents.
items.fix: Always add cheese off the heat. Grate cheese fresh; pre-grated blends poorly.

items.cause: Pasta fully cooked before going in, or sauce too thick.
items.fix: Always undercook the pasta by 2 minutes. The sauce should be pourable before baking; it thickens further in the oven.

items.cause: Too much cheese on top, or oven not hot enough.
items.fix: A mix of breadcrumbs and cheese gives a better crust than cheese alone. Switch to the grill for the last 3 minutes if the top needs more colour.

content.text: American mac and cheese occupies an unusual position in the food culture: it is simultaneously the box dinner of childhood and, in restaurants, a dish chefs compete to make the richest version of. The baked, breadcrumbed version is the one worth making from scratch. It is a Saturday night dish or a weeknight treat that costs almost nothing and is eaten quietly and with commitment.
